endpoints.name: Shocking Future Son Goku
endpoints.incarnation: Son Goku: GT (Black Star Dragon Ball Saga, de-aged child-body)

description: Two cards in the same set (Cross Worlds BT3), adjacent collector numbers (007 + 011), same Bandai-canonical character tag scope (`Son Goku: GT` + `Trunks: GT`), same era label (`Black Star Dragon Ball Saga`), canonically depicting the two principal members of the four-member GT main traveling cast (Goku + Pan + Trunks + Giru) that recovers the Black Star Dragon Balls in Dragon Ball GT's opening arc (Toei Animation, 1996-1997). The set's designer's note at dbs-cardgame.com explicitly cites the cross-franchise GT integration as Cross Worlds' core design intent. This is a designer-confirmed cycle / story-spotlight pairing: same arc, same era, same canonical traveling-companion relation. Bidirectional mirror.
citations: [Dragon Ball Wiki: Black Star Dragon Ball Saga]
voice_note: Two card-endpoints. Both equal nodes. Must be traversable from either side. Hence the mirror. The sigh is professional, not personal. The edge replaces an Edgelord-canonical Connections section above the legacy bbl-researcher ### Related cards section — both stay, the legacy block is for downstream archeology, the new Connections block carries the wikilink-edge that Obsidian and the future bundler can traverse. Quality of one edge >> quantity of weak edges; this is the canonical traveling-companion edge for the GT main cast in our corpus, anchoring two of the four members of the canonical Goku-Pan-Trunks-Giru party.
